_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples responded to environmental processes and changes over the course of their deep history.

_source.w: We are learning about how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples responded to environmental processes and changes over the course of their deep history.
y: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples successfully adapted to massive environmental changes — from ice ages to rising sea levels — over tens of thousands of years.
These responses demonstrate extraordinary resilience, knowledge, and innovation.
Understanding these adaptations challenges the myth that Indigenous Peoples were passive in their relationship with the environment.
eg: Investigating how Aboriginal Peoples adapted to the end of the last Ice Age, including rising sea levels that created new coastlines.
Exploring how cultural burning was used to manage landscapes and promote biodiversity.
Examining how changes in climate affected food sources and led to new technologies and practices.
Discussing oral traditions and songlines that record environmental changes over thousands of years.
Analysing how aquaculture systems like the Budj Bim eel traps demonstrate sophisticated engineering responses to environmental conditions.
Comparing Aboriginal responses to environmental change with those of other ancient societies.
